articles.full_text:
Telangana Latest Weather Update: Temperatures Set to Hit 40°C, Heatwave Alert for Next Four Days
The weather department has warned that Telangana will face intense heat over the next four days. Several districts may see temperatures touching 40°C, raising concerns about heatwave conditions and health risks for residents.
March has arrived, and with it, the real summer heat. The weather department warns that high temperatures, which started on Monday in Telangana, will spread to more areas over the next four days. The Hyderabad weather centre is telling everyone to brace for it.
The weather department has warned that from March 2, districts like Adilabad, Komram Bheem Asifabad, Mahbubnagar, Mancherial, Nagarkurnool, and Nirmal could see temperatures between 36 and 40 degrees. They say this situation will last until March 6, so people in these areas need to be careful.
The heat will intensify in more places. From Wednesday, March 4, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Jogulamba Gadwal, Mulugu, and Wanaparthy will feel the heat. Then from March 5, Kamareddy, Medak, Narayanpet, Nizamabad, and Peddapalli will join the list. A yellow alert is out for these districts, with temps hitting 36 to 40 degrees until March 6.
45
Image Credit : Freepik
This time the heat will be scorching..
The weather department says the March heat is just a sample. The real scorcher will be in April and May, when the sun will be at its fiercest. They predict this year will be much hotter than last year, especially in the old combined districts of Adilabad and Mahbubnagar.
55
Image Credit : freepik
Highest temperatures in Hyderabad
Even Telangana's capital, Hyderabad, won't be spared. The Weatherman says rising temperatures, hot winds, and humidity will trouble city folks. On Tuesday, the city is expected to record a high of 36 to 37 degrees. Meanwhile, East Telangana districts might even touch 40 degrees. Everyone is advised to take proper precautions to get some relief from this heat.
